summary: Decompiled stage-related functions in Ice Mountain and Kongo Jungle. `grIceMt_801F91EC` was promoted from an unidentified/static `FUN_801f91ec` stub to a named exported function with a concrete header prototype, matching 100% according to the report. `grKongo_801D8078` was implemented as an item scan that finds a nearby item of kind `0xDA` relative to a stage-derived position, improving that unmatched item to 98.43%. The PR body excerpt appears unrelated to this PR title/diff, so the reliable evidence is primarily the diff and automated match report.

key_files.reason: Renamed and implemented `grIceMt_801F91EC`; adjusted animation call arguments from `-20` to `0` for matching.
Replaced incorrect/unknown one-argument `UNK_RET grIceMt_801F91EC(HSD_GObj*)` declaration with the full eight-argument `void` prototype including `s16*` and `mpLib_Callback`.
Added include for item helpers and implemented `grKongo_801D8078` by scanning active item GObjs and checking distance against a stage parameter radius.

what_changed: `FUN_801f91ec` in `gricemt.c` was renamed/exported as `grIceMt_801F91EC` and no longer left behind as a `/// #grIceMt_801F91EC` undecompiled marker.

`grIceMt_801F91EC` writes a sequence of parameters into a `s16*` buffer, calls `grAnime_801C7A04` for one or two animation indices, and registers a ground joint callback through `mpJointSetCb1(param_5, gp, param_8)`.
The Ice Mountain animation helper calls changed their fourth argument from `-20` to `0`, which the match report indicates was part of reaching a 100% match for `grIceMt_801F91EC`.
`gricemt.h` now exposes the actual signature for `grIceMt_801F91EC`: `void grIceMt_801F91EC(HSD_GObj*, s16*, int, int, int, int, int, mpLib_Callback)`.
`grkongo.c` now includes `it/it_26B1.h`, enabling calls such as `itGetKind` and `it_8026B294` in the decompiled Kongo function.

`grKongo_801D8078` now calls `Ground_801C4DA0(&pos, &unk)`, iterates `HSD_GObj_Entities->items`, filters items with kind `0xDA`, gets each item position, computes squared 3D distance, compares it to `grKg_804D6980->unk28 * grKg_804D6980->unk28`, and returns the first matching item GObj or `NULL`.
smart_moves: Used squared-distance comparison in `grKongo_801D8078` instead of a square root, matching typical game-code proximity checks and likely preserving instruction shape.

Pulled the Kongo radius value from `grKg_804D6980->unk28` into a local `r` before comparing `dx2 + dy2 + dz2 < r * r`, which keeps the expression clear and compatible with decomp matching constraints.
Separated position deltas and squared components into explicit locals (`dx`, `dy`, `dz`, `dx2`, `dy2`, `dz2`, `r`), a common tactic when matching compiler register/local allocation in decompilation.
Promoted `grIceMt_801F91EC` to a correctly named and externally declared function rather than leaving a local `FUN_` symbol, improving cross-file consistency and searchability.
Fixed the Ice Mountain header prototype at the same time as the implementation, avoiding stale `UNK_RET`/wrong-arity declarations that can mislead later decomp work.
decomp_lessons: When a function takes many untyped integer parameters, even a mostly parameter-name-only implementation can still be valuable if the call sequence, stores, and callback registration match; do not over-infer semantics without stronger evidence.
Header updates are part of matching work: replacing `UNK_RET` and wrong prototypes with concrete signatures helps future callsite cleanup and prevents accidental ABI misunderstandings.
For stage/item interaction functions, check global entity lists such as `HSD_GObj_Entities->items` and item helper APIs (`itGetKind`, `it_8026B294`) before inventing bespoke traversal logic.

Match reports should be interpreted precisely: this PR produced one new 100% match for `grIceMt_801F91EC`, while `grKongo_801D8078` only improved to 98.43%, so the Kongo function may still need final matching cleanup.
The PR body excerpt appears to describe unrelated functions (`IsNameUnique`, `un_80305D00`), so future agents should prioritize diff and automated report evidence when PR metadata is inconsistent.
naming_conventions: Stage functions keep the `gr<Stage>_<address>` form, e.g. `grIceMt_801F91EC` and `grKongo_801D8078`.
Unidentified local names like `FUN_801f91ec` should be replaced with canonical project symbol casing once the function identity is confirmed.
Ground/stage globals retain short stage prefixes, such as `grIm_` for Ice Mountain and `grKg_` for Kongo Jungle.
Unknown fields remain conservatively named, e.g. `grKg_804D6980->unk28`, when the diff does not establish a more semantic name.
Temporary decomp parameter names such as `param_1` through `param_8` remained in `grIceMt_801F91EC`, indicating semantics were not yet strong enough for meaningful names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changing the final argument of `grAnime_801C7A04` from `-20` to `0` was an important matching detail for `grIceMt_801F91EC`.

The Ice Mountain function stores incoming parameters into a `s16*` array in order, starting with `param_2[0] = 0`, then parameter-derived values, which likely mirrors the original stack/register flow.
The Kongo implementation uses explicit item-list traversal via `for (cur = HSD_GObj_Entities->items; cur != NULL; cur = cur->next)`.
The Kongo proximity check avoids `sqrtf` and compares squared distance to squared radius, preserving likely original floating-point operation structure.
The automated report recorded `grIceMt_801F91EC` as a new 176-byte 100% match and `grKongo_801D8078` as a 185-byte improvement to 98.43%, so further Kongo instruction-level tuning may remain.

review_feedback.feedback: Matched code remained at 59.10% overall with +176 bytes; `grIceMt_801F91EC` became a 100% match.
`grKongo_801D8078` improved from 0.00% to 98.43% but was not reported as a full match.
The human text excerpt's summary describes unrelated name-entry and trophy-viewer functions, so it should not be treated as substantive review feedback for this PR.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching `grKongo_801D8078` beyond 98.43%.
Search for callsites of `grIceMt_801F91EC` to rename `param_1`-style arguments based on actual usage.
Investigate item kind `0xDA` in Kongo Jungle to determine whether the hardcoded kind should receive a named enum or constant.
Check whether `grKg_804D6980->unk28` can be given a semantic radius/proximity field name after more Kongo stage data is decompiled.
Audit other `grAnime_801C7A04(..., 7, -20)` versus `0` callsites for similar matching-sensitive constants.
